reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: Posted an updated version of the patch series (v2) addressing feedback from the RFC, including adding a guard for the tracepoint and renaming some functions.

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This allows for hold time estimation and provides the holder's stack at release time, which can differ from what perf lock contention captures.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer Usama Arif pointed out that the patch does not consistently check for blocked waiters, specifically suggesting to use waitqueue_active(&sem->waiters) in percpu_up_write() to match other calls.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello!

I saw that you mentioned in the commmit message that you do this for only
blocked waiters except for percpu_up_write(). We can use
waitqueue_active(&sem->waiters) to check for this over here so that
its consistent with every other call?

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This provides additional information such as hold time estimation and the holder's stack at release time, which can be useful for debugging and performance analysis. The new tracepoint has minimal impact on performance and binary size. It was previously discussed in an RFC (Request For Comments) and this patch addresses some of the feedback received.
